title: MCP Server Connection Lifecycle

state_fields.failed_servers: list of servers that failed to connect
state_fields._failed_server_set: set for O(1) dedup of failed_servers
state_fields.errors: dict[MCPServer, BaseException] — last error per server

activity_flow: START: async with MCPServerManager(servers) as manager
Create asyncio.Task per server calling _attempt_connect(server, raise_on_error=False)
If suppress_cancelled_error=False: re-raise first CancelledError result
If strict=True AND failed_servers not empty:
If not in _failed_server_set: add to connected_servers list
If drop_failed_servers=True: restore active to previous_connected_servers intersection
If server was in failed_servers: _remove_failed_server(server), errors.pop(server)
worker = _get_worker(server)  [create _ServerWorker if not exists or done]
If server not in _failed_server_set: append to failed_servers, add to _failed_server_set
If failed_only=False: await cleanup_all() → servers_to_retry = all_servers, reset failed/errors
BRANCH: connect_in_parallel? (same parallel/sequential path as connect_all)
EXCEPT CancelledError: suppress if suppress_cancelled_error=True, else re-raise; log debug
EXCEPT Exception: log exception, errors[server] = exc
TRY: await worker.cleanup()  [queues 'cleanup' command, awaits future]
If action='cleanup': await _run_with_timeout_in_task(server.cleanup, timeout); EXIT loop
Set future.set_result(None) on success, future.set_exception(exc) on error
key_conditions: connect_in_parallel=True → worker-per-server tasks for concurrent connection; task-affine cleanup
connect_in_parallel=False → sequential connection, timeout via asyncio.timeout or call_later
strict=True → raise on first connect failure (after gathering all parallel results)
drop_failed_servers=True → exclude failed servers from active_servers
suppress_cancelled_error=True → treat CancelledError as soft failure, log and continue
reconnect(failed_only=False) → full cleanup before retry; avoids double-connect via _servers_to_connect filter
_record_failure deduplicates: server only added to failed_servers once even if called multiple times

error_handling.CancelledError_suppressed: recorded as failure, server dropped from active (if drop_failed=True), does not propagate
error_handling.CancelledError_unsuppressed: re-raised, triggers cleanup of all attempted servers
error_handling.Exception_non_strict: recorded via _record_failure, server excluded from active_servers, loop continues
error_handling.BaseException_fatal: always re-raised regardless of strict/suppress settings, triggers cleanup

timeout_mechanism._run_with_timeout_in_task: Uses asyncio.timeout (Python 3.11+) or loop.call_later fallback. Preserves task affinity for AnyIO cancel scope compatibility. Falls back to asyncio.wait_for if not in a task.

parallel_worker_lifecycle._ServerWorker.__init__: creates asyncio.Queue and spawns self._task = asyncio.create_task(self._run())
parallel_worker_lifecycle.connect(): submits 'connect' command to queue, awaits Future resolution
parallel_worker_lifecycle.cleanup(): submits 'cleanup' command to queue, awaits Future; _run() exits after cleanup
parallel_worker_lifecycle.is_done: returns self._task.done(); if True, worker is stale and recreated by _get_worker()

key_relationships: MCPServerManager creates _ServerWorker per MCPServer when connect_in_parallel=True
_ServerWorker wraps MCPServer.connect() and MCPServer.cleanup() with timeout
_ServerCommand is a dataclass queued into _ServerWorker._queue (action, timeout_seconds, future)

properties.all_servers: list[MCPServer] — all managed servers regardless of status
properties.failed_servers: list[MCPServer] — servers that failed last connect attempt
properties.errors: dict[MCPServer, BaseException] — last error per server

business_rules: MCP servers must be connected before agent can use their tools
Agents reference manager.active_servers — only successfully connected servers are exposed
Failed servers tracked in failed_servers/errors for observability and retry
reconnect() supports partial retry (failed_only=True) or full restart (failed_only=False)
Task affinity: connect and cleanup must run in same asyncio task for AnyIO cancel scope correctness; parallel mode uses _ServerWorker tasks to enforce this
